title: Introduction and Research Context
objective: Introduce the problem of pedestrian violating crossing behavior and its significance for traffic safety research

title: Theoretical Background: Theory of Planned Behavior and Extensions
objective: Provide theoretical grounding in TPB and its extensions for understanding pedestrian behavior prediction

title: Structural Equation Modeling Methodology
objective: Describe the SEM methodology employed for modeling pedestrian violating crossing intentions and its analytical advantages

title: Behavioral Factors in Crossing Intentions
objective: Synthesize evidence on instrumental attitude, descriptive norm, conformity tendency, and perceived risk as predictors of violating behavior intentions

title: Demographic and Traffic Context Factors
objective: Analyze the role of demographic characteristics and traffic factors in shaping pedestrian crossing decisions

title: Study Limitations and Methodological Considerations
objective: Discuss self-report bias, cultural generalizability concerns, and measurement limitations in the reviewed research

title: Conclusions and Implications for Safety Interventions
objective: Summarize key findings on TPB extensions for pedestrian behavior prediction and their implications for traffic safety interventions
